findings.title: No authentication on any endpoint
findings.description: GET /, GET /health, POST /locate are all unauthenticated. Any network-accessible client gets full geolocation capability.
findings.exploit_scenario: Attacker on lab network POSTs to /locate with arbitrary receiver geometry and gets transmitter location estimates with no credential required. GET /health reveals model training data sizes.
findings.impact: Full exposure of geolocation capability. DRDO EW system baseline expectation is access control.
findings.recommendation: Add HTTP Basic Auth via FastAPI security dependency, or wrap behind nginx with IP allowlist to lab VLAN.

findings.title: External CDN dependencies: unpkg.com (Leaflet) and CARTO tiles
findings.description: Leaflet CSS/JS loaded from unpkg.com CDN. Map tiles from cartodb.com. Both are live external calls on every page load. Likely to fail in DRDO air-gapped or restricted-network lab.
findings.exploit_scenario: 1) Air-gap: CDN unreachable, UI blank, demo fails. 2) Supply chain: compromised CDN serves malicious Leaflet, exfiltrating geolocation query data from the ops console.
findings.impact: Demo failure in restricted network, or supply-chain compromise of the ops console.
findings.recommendation: Bundle Leaflet locally in api/static/. Mount StaticFiles in FastAPI. For tiles, accept blank map or use offline tile pack.

findings.title: joblib pickle deserialization without integrity verification
findings.description: joblib.load() on four .pkl files at startup. Equivalent to pickle.load() — arbitrary code executes during deserialization. No hash verification before loading.
findings.exploit_scenario: Attacker gains write access to models/saved/ on lab machine. Replaces gp_lat_irtam.pkl with malicious pickle. Service restart executes arbitrary code as service user.
findings.impact: Remote code execution on host machine at service startup.
findings.recommendation: Generate SHA-256 hashes for all .pkl files, commit to models/saved/checksums.json, verify hashes before joblib.load().

findings.title: f107 field missing bounds validation — accepts nan/inf/negative
findings.description: f107: float = 130.0 has no @field_validator. Accepts nan, inf, negative values. These propagate into PyIRTAM and the GP feature vector.
findings.exploit_scenario: Client sends f107=nan. NaN passes range comparisons (NaN < 50 == False). Propagates into GP features array, producing NaN corrected_lat/corrected_lon in response with no error.
findings.impact: Silent corrupted output during demo. NaN in geolocation result presented to DRDO panel.
findings.recommendation: Add @field_validator('f107') checking for isnan/isinf and range 50-400 SFU. Same pattern as other fields.

findings.title: Internal exception details exposed in API response body
findings.description: GP prediction failure returns note=f'GP prediction raised {type(e).__name__}: {e}' in the API response payload. Internal error strings sent to caller.
findings.exploit_scenario: Attacker triggers edge-case GP inputs repeatedly. Response body reveals sklearn error messages, internal feature vector structure, and model library details.
findings.impact: Information disclosure about ML implementation. Enables targeted attacks.
findings.recommendation: Log full error server-side. Return generic 'GP correction unavailable for this request.' to caller.

findings.title: FastAPI /docs, /redoc, /openapi.json enabled — full schema exposed
findings.description: FastAPI default enables Swagger UI at /docs, ReDoc at /redoc, and machine-readable schema at /openapi.json. No authentication on these endpoints.
findings.exploit_scenario: Attacker fetches /openapi.json, gets complete field names, types, validators, and response schemas for a DRDO EW geolocation system.
findings.impact: API schema disclosure. For a defense system, the interface design is itself sensitive.
findings.recommendation: Set docs_url=None, redoc_url=None, openapi_url=None in FastAPI() constructor for the production build.

findings.title: No rate limiting on computationally expensive /locate endpoint
findings.description: POST /locate runs 2 ionospheric model queries + optional subprocess (120s timeout). No per-client throttle, no request queue. Trivially saturatable.
findings.exploit_scenario: 50 concurrent requests. Each runs 2 IRI/IRTAM queries. Service CPU saturates, legitimate demo queries time out.
findings.impact: Service failure during DRDO panel evaluation.
findings.recommendation: Add slowapi rate limiter: @limiter.limit('10/minute') on /locate endpoint.

findings.title: No HTTP security headers on static UI response
findings.description: HTMLResponse for GET / has no Content-Security-Policy, X-Frame-Options, or X-Content-Type-Options headers. Page loads external scripts without CSP restriction.
findings.exploit_scenario: Without CSP, any injected script (via compromised CDN or future inline code) executes unrestricted in the ops console context.
findings.impact: Permissive execution context for scripts in the ops console.
findings.recommendation: Add security headers to HTMLResponse via response.headers. Restrict script-src to 'self' after bundling Leaflet locally.

findings.title: No lockfile — non-reproducible dependency installs
findings.description: requirements.txt uses >= version pins only. No pip.lock or equivalent. Installs on DRDO lab machine may pull different versions than tested.
findings.exploit_scenario: CVE published in fastapi or scikit-learn after development but before DRDO install. Lab machine pulls vulnerable version silently.
findings.impact: Unverified dependency versions on deployment machine.
findings.recommendation: Run 'pip freeze > requirements.lock' from working environment and commit. Install with 'pip install -r requirements.lock' on DRDO machine.

findings.title: /health exposes gp_training_rows — internal training data shape
findings.description: GET /health returns gp_training_rows: {irtam: N, sami3: 229}. Reveals training population sizes to any unauthenticated caller.
findings.exploit_scenario: Caller learns storm-time GP trained on 229 rows (small population). Crafts inputs to probe generalization boundary, identifying accuracy limits.
findings.impact: Information disclosure about model training data volume and distribution.
findings.recommendation: Remove gp_training_rows from /health for production. Keep boolean flags only: gp_models_loaded, gp_models: {irtam: bool, sami3: bool}.

findings.title: Hardcoded developer filesystem paths in ACHAIM_EXE_PATH fallback
findings.description: ACHAIM_EXE_PATH falls back to 'C:/Users/prith/Downloads/A-CHAIM_User_Release-6.0.3/...'. Path won't exist on DRDO lab machine. A-CHAIM silently falls back to IRI without indication.
findings.exploit_scenario: DRDO lab machine runs service without ACHAIM_EXE_PATH set. Panel tests high-latitude scenario. Service uses IRI silently. Results are wrong with no warning.
findings.impact: Silent capability degradation. High-latitude branch (A-CHAIM) never fires.
findings.recommendation: Remove hardcoded fallback. Set ACHAIM_EXE_PATH='' as default, log explicit warning when unset and A-CHAIM is selected.

findings.title: A-CHAIM executable path controlled via env var — tamper risk in shared lab
findings.description: ACHAIM_EXE_PATH env var controls subprocess binary path. shell=False prevents shell injection but alternate executable could be specified. os.path.isfile() check is partial protection.
findings.exploit_scenario: Pre-service-start env modification points ACHAIM_EXE_PATH to a different legitimate executable. subprocess.run() executes it with service account permissions.
findings.impact: Arbitrary execution as service user in shared-environment attack scenario.
findings.recommendation: Run service as dedicated low-privilege service account. Restrict env var modification. A-CHAIM only triggers at |lat| >= 60 — India demos unaffected.

findings.title: IRTAM coefficient cache directory permissions not verified
findings.description: PyIRTAM reads coefficient files from package data directory. Default Windows ACLs on non-admin installs may leave directory world-writable. Concurrent process could modify coefficients.
findings.exploit_scenario: Another process on lab machine replaces IRTAM coefficient files. Model accuracy degrades silently. No integrity check on coefficient reads.
findings.impact: Model accuracy compromise without detection. Defense system producing wrong geolocation estimates.
findings.recommendation: Verify PyIRTAM coefficient directory ACLs post-install. Run service as dedicated account with read-only access to package directories.
